note: Criterion IDs and one-line authored summaries. Full text in AICPA TSP Section 100 PDF.

controls.title: Board demonstrates commitment to integrity and ethical values
Board independent of management; exercises oversight of internal controls
Management establishes reporting structures and authorities
Competence policies established for roles that affect internal controls
Accountability enforced for internal control responsibilities
COSO principle: uses relevant quality information to achieve objectives
Internal communication of control information and responsibilities
Communication with external parties on matters affecting controls
Objectives specified with sufficient clarity to identify related risks
Risks to achievement of objectives identified and analyzed
Fraud risk assessed including risk of management override
Changes that could affect the system of internal controls identified
Ongoing and separate evaluations to ascertain control effectiveness
Control deficiencies evaluated and communicated to responsible parties
Control activities selected and developed to mitigate risks
Technology controls selected and developed to achieve objectives
Controls deployed through policies and procedures
Logical and physical access controls limit access to system assets
New internal and external users provisioned with appropriate access
Access to system assets removed when no longer required
Physical access to facilities restricted and monitored
Assets protected from unauthorized access and environmental threats
Boundaries of system protect against unauthorized external access
Restricted access to confidential information during transmission
Unauthorized software prevented from accessing system resources
Vulnerabilities detected through monitoring of configurations
Anomalies and security incidents detected through monitoring
Identified security incidents evaluated and responded to
Security incidents contained; impact documented; root cause analyzed
Incident response communications to affected parties and regulators
Changes to infrastructure, data, software and procedures managed
Business disruption risks identified and mitigation strategies assessed
Business partners and vendors assessed and managed for risk
Current processing capacity and usage monitored and evaluated
Environmental, regulatory and technological changes that affect availability evaluated
Recovery plan to restore availability in case of disruption
Confidential information identified and designated
Confidential information protected during retention and disposal
Processing complete, accurate and timely per system requirements
System inputs validated and errors corrected
System processing errors detected and corrected
System output complete, accurate and distributed to authorized parties
Stored data protected, retained, and disposed in accordance with requirements
Privacy notice provided to data subjects about collection and use
Personal information collected only with consent or lawful basis
Personal information collected limited to that which is relevant
Sensitive personal information collected only with explicit consent
Personal information used only for purposes for which collected
Personal information retained for no longer than necessary
Obsolete personal information destroyed or anonymized
Data subjects can access, review and correct their information
Personal information corrected or erased upon verified request
Personal information disclosed only to authorized parties
Disclosures authorized and limited to stated purposes
Third parties informed of privacy commitments; compliance monitored
Individuals notified of breaches affecting their personal information
Unsolicited personal information handled per privacy notice
Requests from data subjects to correct or erase information honored
Personal information transferred to third parties per agreements
Personal information quality maintained per standards
Inquiries and complaints from data subjects addressed promptly
